rationale: Targeted check of the CR-001 fix-up diff only (no full re-review). CR-001 resolved: a new _sanitize_log_value strips CR/LF/control chars ([\r\n\x00-\x1f\x7f]) from the five client-controlled fields (auth_config_id, target_origin, post_message_error, bridge_error_code, idp_error_code) before they are formatted into the log line, so the unauthenticated endpoint is self-contained-safe regardless of logger/env. A new test asserts no \r/\n survives in the logged message and that injected text is flattened (38 bridge tests pass, ruff clean). The fix-up introduces no new high-risk issue (regex + pure helper + test only), so no confirmation pass was needed. business_review and standards_review carried forward from code-review-final.json.

business_review.criterion: AC2 — success-page-served log includes exact target_origin
AC3 — listener logs windowOrigin+apiOrigin and any mcp_auth_callback message
AC4 — beacon not blocked by CSP for success and error
AC5 — no secret values in any new log/beacon
business_review.notes: carried forward; sanitization further hardens field handling

business_review.criterion: AC6 — no behavioral change to the auth flow

standards_review.notes: carried forward; fix-up commit also EPMCDME-13237-prefixed
carried forward; small pure helper, ruff clean
CR-001 resolved by source-level CR/LF/control-char sanitization; was partial in the final round

finding_status.notes: Source-level _sanitize_log_value applied to all five client-controlled log fields; regression test asserts CR/LF neutralized. Endpoint no longer relies on the logger's incidental json escaping.
